message:
Implement minimal functional TUI with terminal lifecycle management

Add working Ratatui-based terminal user interface with proper crossterm
initialization, event loop, graceful teardown, and basic placeholder
rendering, establishing the foundation for interactive search UI.

run_tui() function lifecycle:
Entry point called from lib.rs Commands::Tui branch, orchestrates
complete TUI session from terminal setup through teardown:

1. Terminal initialization sequence:
   - enable_raw_mode(): Disable line buffering and canonical input mode,
     capture Ctrl+C (not terminate), hide cursor, enable ANSI escape
     code processing for Ratatui drawing commands
   - EnterAlternateScreen: Switch to alternate screen buffer preserving
     user's shell scrollback (critical: exiting TUI restores original
     screen contents rather than leaving TUI artifacts in history)
   - CrosstermBackend::new(stdout): Create Ratatui backend wrapping
     stdout for platform-neutral terminal control (same code works on
     Windows cmd.exe, PowerShell, Unix terminals via crossterm
     abstraction layer)
   - Terminal::new(backend): Allocate Ratatui Terminal handle managing
     double-buffered rendering (backend writes to hidden buffer,
     terminal.draw() swaps buffers atomically to eliminate flicker)

2. Event loop with tick-based updates:
   - last_tick tracking: Instant::now() baseline for periodic UI refresh
     independent of user input, enables progress bar animation and
     background indexing status updates even when user idle
   - tick_rate: 250ms interval for balanced responsiveness (4 FPS refresh
     for smooth progress indicators without excessive CPU usage)
   - crossterm::event::poll(timeout): Non-blocking event check with
     dynamic timeout computed as tick_rate minus elapsed time since
     last_tick, ensures tick fires at regular intervals while remaining
     responsive to keypresses (poll returns immediately on key event,
     timeout ensures periodic wake-up for redraws)

3. Rendering placeholder UI (terminal.draw closure):
   - Block widget with title "coding-agent-search (press q to quit)"
     and Borders::ALL (top/bottom/left/right box drawing characters)
   - Fills entire terminal area (f.area() gives available space after
     accounting for status bars, reserved rows)
   - Future commits will replace with three-pane layout: top search bar,
     left results list, right conversation detail per PLAN § 6.2

4. Input handling (crossterm::event::read):
   - Poll with timeout (computed from tick_rate) blocks until key press
     or timeout expires
   - If key event received, check for quit keys: 'q' or Esc
   - Use let-chain pattern (let Event::Key && matches!) for concise
     event filtering without nested if/match
   - Break from loop on quit, falling through to teardown

5. Tick update logic:
   - After processing events, check if tick_rate elapsed since last_tick
   - If so, reset last_tick to Instant::now() for next interval
   - No-op in placeholder implementation, but establishes hook point
     for future features:
     - Poll crossbeam channel for SearchResult messages from background
       search thread, update app_state.search_results
     - Poll for IndexProgress messages, update app_state.indexing_progress
     - Trigger terminal.draw() to reflect new state (current implementation
       draws unconditionally at top of loop, future optimization will
       draw only on state change)

6. Teardown (teardown_terminal helper):
   - disable_raw_mode(): Restore canonical input processing, re-enable
     line buffering, show cursor
   - LeaveAlternateScreen: Flip back to main screen buffer, restore
     user's shell prompt at exact position before TUI launch (user sees
     command they typed to launch TUI, followed by shell prompt, not
     leftover TUI artifacts)
   - Both operations critical for clean exit: if program panics without
     calling teardown, terminal left in unusable state (raw mode,
     alternate screen) requiring user to run `reset` command

Error handling and panic safety:
All terminal operations wrapped in Result, propagate errors to caller
(lib.rs run() -> main()). If error during initialization (e.g.,
stdout not a TTY, permission denied on /dev/tty), run_tui() returns
Err with anyhow context, main() prints error and exits gracefully.

Panic safety not yet implemented (PLAN § 6.2 panic hook requirement):
Future commit will add std::panic::set_hook before enable_raw_mode to
ensure teardown_terminal() called even on panic/unwrap, preventing
broken terminal state. Without hook, panic inside draw() closure or
event::read() leaves terminal in raw mode requiring manual reset.

Crossterm vs termion trade-offs:
Using crossterm (not termion) for backend because:
- Windows support: crossterm works on cmd.exe and PowerShell natively,
  termion is Unix-only
- Mouse events: crossterm provides richer mouse handling (future feature
  for click-to-select conversations)
- Maintained: crossterm actively developed, termion maintenance sporadic
Downside: crossterm slightly higher overhead than termion (extra
abstraction layer), but negligible for TUI refresh rates (250ms ticks).

Terminal size handling:
f.area() automatically tracks terminal size, updates on resize events
(crossterm sends Event::Resize on SIGWINCH). Ratatui layout engine
reflows widgets to fit new dimensions without application code changes.
Future three-pane layout will use ratatui::layout::Constraint::Percentage
for responsive sizing (e.g., left pane 40%, right pane 60%, auto-shrink
on narrow terminals).

Missing features for MVP (future commits per PLAN § 14.3):
- Search input box with cursor rendering and text editing
- Results list widget with dummy/hardcoded conversations for layout
  validation
- Detail pane with message rendering and tabs (Messages/Snippets/Raw)
- Hotkey handlers (/ for search, f for filters, etc.)
- App state struct (search_query, results, selected_idx, focus)
- Channel integration for background search/index threads
Current implementation only demonstrates terminal lifecycle—placeholder
Block widget proves TUI works (compiles, renders, accepts input, exits
cleanly) before adding complex state management.

Testing TUI interactively:
```
cargo run -- tui
# Expect: full-screen box with title, press 'q' to exit
# Verify: terminal restored to original state after exit
# Test Esc key also quits
# Test Ctrl+C terminates (raw mode captures, but SIGINT still works)
```

Related: PLAN_TO_MAKE_CODING_AGENT_SESSION_SEARCH.md § 6.1 (terminal
handling), § 6.2 (layout, panic hook requirements), § 3.2 (threading
model with event loop), lib.rs (Commands::Tui dispatch), Cargo.toml
(ratatui, crossterm dependencies).
